purpose: Compare two contracts that make cross-section replacement structurally impossible or locally detectable before another full Gemma screen.

selection.rationale: The slice contains both prior malformed-JSON patterns, the strongest observed Spanish and French section-copying cases, and English, German, and Portuguese controls across Apple, Qwen, and Voxtral TTS plus all three STT backends.

execution.randomness: temperature zero and seed zero; repeated output remains an explicit determinism check

transport_contract_amendment.trigger: The committed six-target pilot for each variant returned a single, otherwise isolated Markdown json fence in 12/12 responses.
transport_contract_amendment.decision: Revision 2 accepts either bare JSON or exactly one json code fence with no surrounding text. This changes only deterministic transport decoding; all complete-text, exact-patch, edit-rate, and quality gates remain unchanged.
transport_contract_amendment.non_relaxation: Patch duplicates, no-op patches, extra fields, excessive patch counts, malformed JSON, and any text outside the one allowed envelope remain strict failures.

decision_rule: Promote a variant to the 180-document factorial screen only if every gate passes. If neither passes, retain raw ASR output and continue with stronger local or hosted candidates rather than weakening the gates.
claim_limit: This is a deliberately selected development challenge set. It can reject unsafe prompt and contract variants but cannot estimate release quality, select a safety threshold, or replace held-out human podcast and audiobook evidence.
